overview: Both the API key and System Id will be required to access all API services

sections.title: Authentication
paragraphs: The following HTTP Headers need to be set to authenticate the service request.
Alternatively, the parameters key and sid may be passed as part of a GET request.
lists: X-Pvoutput-Apikey
X-Pvoutput-SystemId

overview: The following rate limits applies per API key for all request types -
The following rate limits applies per API key to Get Statistic API -
Rate limit information can be obtained by setting the HTTP header X-Rate-Limit to 1
The HTTP response will be returned with the additional headers -
The following errors may be encountered across all API services -

sections.title: Method Not Allowed 405: POST or GET only
paragraphs: Data must be sent via the HTTP POST or GET method
lists: Data must be sent via the HTTP POST or GET method

sections.title: Unauthorized 401: Invalid System ID
paragraphs: The required parameter X-Pvoutput-SystemId or sid is missing from the request. The sid is a number which identifies a system. The sid can be obtained from the Settings page under Registered Systems
lists: The required parameter X-Pvoutput-SystemId or sid is missing from the request. The sid is a number which identifies a system. The sid can be obtained from the Settings page under Registered Systems

sections.title: Unauthorized 401: Invalid API Key
paragraphs: The API key is missing in the header request or the API key is invalid.
lists: The API key is missing in the header request or the API key is invalid.

sections.title: Unauthorized 401: Disabled API Key
paragraphs: The API key has not been enabled in the Settings.
lists: The API key has not been enabled in the Settings.

sections.title: Forbidden 403: Read only key
paragraphs: The API key provided is a read only key and cannot access the requested service which updates system data, use the standard key to update system data.
lists: The API key provided is a read only key and cannot access the requested service which updates system data, use the standard key to update system data.

sections.title: Unauthorized 401: Missing, invalid or inactive api key information (X-Pvoutput-Apikey)
paragraphs: The sid and key combination is invalid
lists: The sid and key combination is invalid

sections.title: Forbidden 403: Exceeded number requests per hour
paragraphs: The maximum number of requests per hour has been reached for the API key. Wait till the next hour before making further requests.
lists: The maximum number of requests per hour has been reached for the API key. Wait till the next hour before making further requests.

sections.title: Forbidden 403: Donation Mode
paragraphs: Request is only available in Donation mode.
lists: Request is only available in Donation mode.

services.name: Add Output Service

successSections.title: Success Messages
paragraphs: The following messages will be returned upon successfully adding or updating output data.

subsections.title: OK 200: Added Output
paragraphs: The output data has been recieved and will either be added or updated.
lists: The output data has been recieved and will either be added or updated.

errors.title: Bad request 400: Date [date] invalid
details: The date is incorrectly formatted, expecting yyyymmdd format
The date is incorrectly formatted, expecting yyyymmdd format

errors.title: Bad request 400: Date [date] too old
details: The date must be after 2000-01-01
The date must be after 2000-01-01

errors.title: Bad request 400: Date [date] too new
details: The date must not be a future date
The date must not be a future date

errors.title: Bad request 400: Generation [export] too high for system size [size] on [date]
details: The generation amount to too high compared to the system size
The generation amount to too high compared to the system size

errors.title: Bad request 400: Export [export] too high for system size [size] on [date]
details: The export amount to too high compared to the system size
The export amount to too high compared to the system size

errors.title: Bad request 400: Export [export] cannot exceed generation [generation] by 15%
details: The export amount is too high compared to the generation amount
The export amount is too high compared to the generation amount

errors.title: Bad request 400: Consumption [consumption] too high on [date]
details: Consumption exceeded the 999999999Wh limit
Consumption exceeded the 999999999Wh limit

errors.title: Bad request 400: Peak power [power] too high for system size [size] on [date]
details: The peak power is 50% greater than the system size.
The peak power is 50% greater than the system size.

errors.title: Bad request 400: Min/Max temp missing on [date]
details: Both min and max temperature must exist or both should be omitted
Both min and max temperature must exist or both should be omitted

restrictions: Generated or consumption must be provided for a new output.
Generated and consumption are optional for an update.
If a Time of Export parameter ep , eo , es or eh is set then the existing total export value e is overwritten with the sum of all 4 time of export values.
Min temperature must be lower or equal to max temperature.
Temperature range is -100 to 100 degrees celsius.
Batching is only available with Donation mode enabled.
Maximum batch size is 100.

sections.title: POST Method
paragraphs: The POST method accepts either a single CSV data parameter or multiple parameters. It is recommended to use the POST method as it does not expose the API key via the URL.

sections.title: Parameters
paragraphs: The following parameters are used when requesting the service via GET or POST.
1 Required if the output does not already exist.

Cloudy
Showers

paragraphs: The data input contains comma separated values of the output details. The fields must be passed in the order shown below.

paragraphs: Multiple outputs may be added or updated in a single request by separating each output with a semi-colon in the data input.

errors.title: Bad request 400: Missing energy and power values
details: The request must send at least one of the parameters v1 , v2 , v3 or v4
The request must send at least one of the parameters v1 , v2 , v3 or v4

errors.title: Bad request 400: Invalid future [date]
details: The date parameter must not be greater than the current date.
The date parameter must not be greater than the current date.

details: The date parameter must not be older than 14 days from the current date.
The date parameter must not be older than 14 days from the current date.

details: The power parameter is 50% greater than the system size.
The power parameter is 50% greater than the system size.

details: The system cannot generate power during the hours of the day where there is no sunlight. Timezone may be incorrect.
The system cannot generate power during the hours of the day where there is no sunlight. Timezone may be incorrect.

errors.title: Bad request 400: Energy value [energy] too high for [time]
details: The energy parameter exceeds the 1.0-4.0kWh/kW thresholds between 6:00am and 8:00am
The energy parameter exceeds the 1.0-4.0kWh/kW thresholds between 6:00am and 8:00am

errors.title: Bad request 400: Energy value [energy] too high for system size [system_size]
details: The energy parameter exceeds the 12kWh/kW threshold.
The energy parameter exceeds the 12kWh/kW threshold.

errors.title: Bad Request 400: Invalid net and cumulative
details: Data cannot be both cumulative and net. Remove c1 and/or net from the request.
Data cannot be both cumulative and net. Remove c1 and/or net from the request.

errors.title: Bad Request 400: Missing net power value
details: The request is missing a power value for v2 or v4
The request is missing a power value for v2 or v4

restrictions: The d date parameter must be not be older than 14 days from the current date.
The t time parameter is rounded to the nearest Status Interval configured for the system.
The energy parameters v1 and v3 are measured in watt hours (Wh).
The power parameters v2 and v4 are measured in watts (W).
An energy value cannot be lower than the previous energy value recorded in time.
At least one of the values v1 , v2 , v3 or v4 must be present.
Maximum energy consumption v3 value is 200,000Wh
Maximum power consumption v4 value is 100,000W

sections.title: Parameters
paragraphs: This service accepts both GET or POST requests with the following parameters -

sections.title: Power and Energy Calculation
paragraphs: Power can energy values can be derived from one another. When a system sends only power values v2 and v4 the corresponding energy values are automatically calculated.
Similarly when only energy values v1 and v3 are sent, PVOutput will calculate the average power.

sections.title: Cumulative Energy
paragraphs: The following values are valid for the c1 flag.
1 - Both v1 and v3 values are lifetime energy values.
2 - Only v1 generation is a lifetime energy value.
3 - Only v3 consumption is a lifetime energy value.
lists: 1 - Both v1 and v3 values are lifetime energy values.
2 - Only v1 generation is a lifetime energy value.
3 - Only v3 consumption is a lifetime energy value.

sections.title: Net Data
paragraphs: The n parameter when set to 1 will indicate that the power values passed are net export/import rather than gross generation/consumption. This option is used for devices that are unable to report gross consumption data. The provided import/export data is merged with existing generation data to derive consumption.
Only the power values v2 (export) and v4 (import) are used to process a request with Net Data. Net data can have negative values to signify the opposite direction.
The following table shows how negative and positive values are interpreted, and the resulting import/export value that will be used.

sections.title: Battery Data
paragraphs: The b1 parameter represents the current battery power in watts.
A positive value indicates charging either from generation or load (consumption). A negative value is used for discharge, when battery power is drawn for consumption.
When sending battery data the b1 is parameter is mandatory, other battery parameters will be ignored if b1 is missing.

subsections.title: State Codes
paragraphs: The following values are valid b2 Battery State codes.
lists: 0 - Invalid
1 - Standby
2 - Thermal Management
3 - Enabled
4 - Fault
5-9 - User Defined

sections.title: Examples
paragraphs: Send an energy value of 1000Wh and power value of 150W on 01/Dec/11 at 10:00AM

paragraphs: The following comma delimited data is returned in the message body
The data specification is shown below

subsections.title: Status Codes
paragraphs: 0 - The status did not add or update any data
lists: 1 - The status was successfully added
0 - The status did not add or update any data

restrictions: All restrictions and limitations of the Add Status service.
A maximum of 30 statuses can be sent in a single batch request.
An Add Output is called once for the last successful status update in the batch.
The date parameter must be not be older than 14 days from the current date.
All Net statuses in the batch must have the same date.
A Net status must have export and import data, 0W should be sent if no export/import is to be recorded.

services.name: Get Status Service

successSections.title: Success Messages
paragraphs: The following comma delimited data is returned in the message body
Response with Extended Data request ext=1
The data specification is shown below
Use the d parameter to request a specific date. When date is omitted, the last known live data in the last 7 days will be used.

errors.title: Unauthorized 401: Inaccessible System ID
details: This error is reported when another system id is requested sid1 from an account without donation enabled.
This error is reported when another system id is requested sid1 from an account without donation enabled.

restrictions: The value NaN will be returned where a numeric value does not exist
The maximum number of history status records returned is 288 (1 day).
The parameter sid1 is able to retrieve generation data from any system. Consumption data is not returned. The requesting system must have Donation mode enabled.
Extended Data values (v7-v12) will only be returned when ext is set to 1. The requesting system must have donation mode enabled.
Where no limit is specified a default limit of 30 records applies
Statuses are returned in descending order by default e.g. 5:00PM to 1:00PM. Use the asc parameter to reverse the order
The from and to parameters can be used to restrict the data returned to the specified time range

sections.title: History Query
paragraphs: The h parameter when set to 1 returns the entire status for a given date. If a time parameter is passed then only statuses after the given time will be returned.
Each status is separated by a semi-colon, the data specification is shown below
The example below shows 3 history statuses returned.
To retrieve a full day of live data, use history with a limit of 288 i.e. h=1&limit=288

sections.title: Day Statistics
paragraphs: The stats parameter when set to 1 returns generation, consumption and temperature summary stats for the requested day.
The following parameters can be used with stats enabled -
The data specification is shown below
1 Section only appears for system owners
2 Section only appears for systems with temperature
Example of day statistics response below

to
sid1

sections.title: Insolation
paragraphs: When the insolation parameter is set to 1 , the service will return the total daily insolation value for each output date. This value is identical to the last energy figure returned in the Get Insolation service.

services.name: Get Insolation Service

successSections.title: Success Messages
paragraphs: The following comma delimited data is returned in the message body -
The data specification is shown below -
Insolation values are returned for each 5-minute interval and are separated by a semi-colon ;

restrictions: The UTC timezone is used when the system does not have a timezone configured or no tz parameter is passed.
Insolation data is not available for systems without a configured location or postcode.

sections.title: Parameters
paragraphs: This service accepts GET requests with the following parameters

sections.title: Latitude and Longitude
paragraphs: The latitude and longitude is obtained in the following order of precedence -
The latitude/longitude value in the ll parameter.
The system latitude/longitude configured for the system.
The postcode/zipcode latitude/longitude configured for the system
lists: The latitude/longitude value in the ll parameter.
The system latitude/longitude configured for the system.
The postcode/zipcode latitude/longitude configured for the system

sections.title: Timezone
paragraphs: The timezone set for the system is used for insolation calculation. A different timezone may be used with the tz parameter.

sections.title: Date
paragraphs: The current date is used for insolation calculation. A different date may be used with the d parameter.

sections.title: Example Requests
paragraphs: Get the insolation data for your system for today
Get the insolation data for your system on 28 Feb 2021, in timezone Australia/Brisbane at location -27.4676,153.0279
Get the insolation data for System Id 12345 for today
Replace Your-API-Key and Your-System-Id with your key and system id

sections.title: Success Messages
paragraphs: The following comma delimited data is returned in the message body -
The data specification is shown below -
Insolation values are returned for each 5-minute interval and are separated by a semi-colon ;

sections.title: Restrictions and Limitations
paragraphs: The UTC timezone is used when the system does not have a timezone configured or no tz parameter is passed.
Insolation data is not available for systems without a configured location or postcode.
lists: The UTC timezone is used when the system does not have a timezone configured or no tz parameter is passed.
Insolation data is not available for systems without a configured location or postcode.

services.name: Search Service

successSections.title: Success Messages
paragraphs: The following comma delimited data is returned in the message body -
The data specification is shown below -
N, W, E, S, NE, NW, SE, SW
Each system in the search result is separated by a new line.

rows: N, W, E, S, NE, NW, SE, SW

restrictions: Distance is returned if an origin is given via the distance query 4000 5km or latitude/longitude.
Systems are returned by distance, if available, otherwise system name.
The system latitude and longitude is returned if known, otherwise the postcode latitude/longitude where the system resides is returned.
A maximum of 30 systems will be returned.

sections.title: Parameters
paragraphs: This service accepts GET or POST requests with the following parameters -
1 The point of origin used for distance searches
2 The country name is prefixed to the postcode returned e.g. ‘Australia 2000’
3 Filter results by country code. Required for postcode distance search e.g. ‘4000 20km’
4 Filter results by last output activity. e.g. ‘7’ for the last 7 days
lists: 1 The point of origin used for distance searches
2 The country name is prefixed to the postcode returned e.g. ‘Australia 2000’
3 Filter results by country code. Required for postcode distance search e.g. ‘4000 20km’
4 Filter results by last output activity. e.g. ‘7’ for the last 7 days

subsections.title: Country Codes
lists: au
be
dk
it
nl
uk
us

sections.title: Success Messages
paragraphs: The following comma delimited data is returned in the message body -
The data specification is shown below -
N, W, E, S, NE, NW, SE, SW
Each system in the search result is separated by a new line.

rows: N, W, E, S, NE, NW, SE, SW

sections.title: Restrictions and Limitations
paragraphs: Distance is returned if an origin is given via the distance query 4000 5km or latitude/longitude.
Systems are returned by distance, if available, otherwise system name.
The system latitude and longitude is returned if known, otherwise the postcode latitude/longitude where the system resides is returned.
A maximum of 30 systems will be returned.

services.name: Register Notification Service
restrictions: The alert type must be enabled for the notification to be sent.
All notification types are sent to the same url with the same appid .
The url must not be longer than 150 characters.
The appid must not be longer than 100 characters.
A maximum of 1 application id can be registered for a system.
donationFeatures: Maximum number of application ids increased to 5 .

sections.title: Parameters
paragraphs: This service accepts GET requests with the following parameters -

sections.title: Callback URL
paragraphs: Normally when an alert is triggered the system owner will be sent an email.
If the alert has been registered for notifications then the subject of the email message will be sent to the callback url.
The POST request will contain the following parameters -
The application id in the registration request
Your system has been idle for more than 15 minutes
